formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Accountancy - Applications of Computers in Accounting:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Definition of Accounting",
    "formula": "Accounting is the process of recording, classifying, and reporting financial transactions of a business.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting is a language of business and helps in decision-making."
  },
  {
    "name": "Definition of Accounting Information System (AIS)",
    "formula": "An Accounting Information System is a system that processes accounting data and provides financial information to management.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "AIS is a sub-system of the overall management information system."
  },
  {
    "name": "Benefits of Accounting Information System",
    "formula": "Increased accuracy, reduced time and cost, improved decision-making, and better control.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "AIS provides various benefits to a business."
  },
  {
    "name": "Components of Accounting Information System",
    "formula": "Input, Processing, Storage, Output, and Control.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "AIS consists of five main components."
  },
  {
    "name": "Advantages of Computerized Accounting System (CAS)",
    "formula": "Increased accuracy, reduced time and cost, improved decision-making, and better control.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "CAS provides various benefits to a business."
  },
  {
    "name": "Disadvantages of Computerized Accounting System (CAS)",
    "formula": "High initial cost, dependence on technology, security risks, and user error.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "CAS also has some limitations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Types of Accounting Software",
    "formula": "Single-user, multi-user, and networked system.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting software can be categorized into three types."
  },
  {
    "name": "Features of Accounting Software",
    "formula": "General ledger, accounts payable, accounts receivable, inventory control, and payroll.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting software provides various features."
  },
  {
    "name": "Advantages of Accounting Software",
    "formula": "Increased accuracy, reduced time and cost, improved decision-making, and better control.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting software provides various benefits to a business."
  },
  {
    "name": "Disadvantages of Accounting Software",
    "formula": "High initial cost, dependence on technology, security risks, and user error.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting software also has some limitations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Types of Accounting Hardware",
    "formula": "Input devices (keyboard, scanner), processing devices (computer), and output devices (printer, monitor).",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting hardware can be categorized into three types."
  },
  {
    "name": "Network Types in Accounting",
    "formula": "Local Area Network (LAN), Wide Area Network (WAN), and Metropolitan Area Network (MAN).",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting networks can be categorized into three types."
  },
  {
    "name": "Network Hardware in Accounting",
    "formula": "Router, hub, switch, and modem.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting networks use various hardware components."
  },
  {
    "name": "Network Software in Accounting",
    "formula": "Operating system, network protocol, and network application.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Accounting networks use various software components."
  }
]
```

Note: As the chapter "Applications of Computers in Accounting" does not involve any mathematical formulas, the content provided above consists of key statements and laws related to the topic.
